_doc: Root-level SSOT for the Molecule credential/privilege model: the TYPES of
credential a box or process can hold, and the FORMAT of each — the canonical
env var name, accepted aliases, what it grants, and where it may live. Every
other credential-touching surface derives from this: core sets these env keys,
the workspace runtime forwards a subset into the privileged management MCP, the
mcp-server reads them, and privileged admin clients consume route-scoped keys.
The concierge AUTH_ERROR (2026-07-09) happened because
those three used different NAMES for the org credential with nothing forcing
agreement — this contract is that forcing function. NOT mcp-scoped: the mgmt-MCP
env subset (management_mcp_env) is just one CONSUMER of the taxonomy below.
Most legacy privilege POWER remains coarse (org-api-key == admin == full tenant
admin); cp-promote is the deliberate exception, scoped to exactly two production routes.
See contracts/boot-token for the
boot credential's wire format and contracts/object-store for the restore route.

credentials.grants: authenticates exactly ONE workspace to its tenant workspace-server (register / heartbeat / files / a2a / list_peers). Workspace-scoped; NOT admin.
credentials.format: opaque 256-bit base64url bearer; sha256(plaintext) stored (workspace_auth_tokens, kind=instance); NOT a JWT

disclosure.notes: The plaintext exists ONLY in the mint response — core stores sha256(plaintext) and cannot re-issue it. The operator-facing re-show path (GET /workspaces/:id/external/connection) therefore returns auth_token="" and MUST stamp disclosure.unavailable_marker at every credential site. See the top-level `disclosure` block: material that PERSISTS this credential has to fail closed on the marker, or a re-shown paste overwrites the holder's only copy.

credentials.setter: molecule-core wsauth.IssueToken → /configs/.auth_token (in-container SSOT) or MOLECULE_WORKSPACE_TOKEN env (external runtimes)

credentials.grants: FULL tenant admin over the org, exercised through the management MCP (create/delete workspaces, mint/revoke org tokens, secrets, plugins). Same power surface as the break-glass admin token.
credentials.format: opaque bearer; sha256-hashed in org_api_tokens; named, expiring, revocable, audited; per-org mint-ceiling
credentials.holder: the concierge (default) and any agent a user grants an org token
credentials.reader: molecule-mcp-server management surface — src/tools/management/client.ts (Authorization: Bearer). STRICT: no alias accepted.
credentials.notes: This is the CANONICAL org credential. The unprefixed ORG_API_KEY is a DEPRECATED alias (declared in an older runtime allowlist, set/read by nobody) — do NOT use it.

credentials.grants: CP-admin endpoints + the default (non-management) mcp-server tool surface; ALSO the mcp-server startup auth-preflight credential.
credentials.notes: DISTINCT from org-api-key and must NOT be collapsed: preflight validates MOLECULE_API_KEY, but management tools authenticate with MOLECULE_ORG_API_KEY — so a server can boot 'connected' yet fail every management call. Both must be delivered.

credentials.grants: FULL tenant admin (break-glass) + doubles as the tenant→control-plane identity (X-Molecule-Admin-Token).
credentials.format: infra-minted per-tenant hex; PLAINTEXT in org_instances.admin_token + Secrets Manager; non-expiring, non-revocable (rotate only by reprovision)
credentials.holder: tenant platform box + control-plane; and (as MOLECULE_ADMIN_TOKEN) the concierge/platform box ONLY
credentials.notes: The un-managed ROOT of the org (same power as an org-api-key, different origin/lifecycle). WS-B: ADMIN_TOKEN and MOLECULE_ADMIN_TOKEN are hard-forbidden from ordinary workspace env (core + CP privileged-env denylists). Prefer a managed org-api-key wherever a credential is handed to an agent.

credentials.grants: control-plane super-tier admin (the mcp-server cp_admin.* tools).
credentials.holder: tenant platform box (from the bootstrap bundle)

credentials.grants: only the exact production HTTP routes enumerated in http_grants; no other control-plane authority
credentials.holder: production control-plane validator, operator-launched privileged management MCP, or authorized release-operator CLI only
credentials.setter: value SSOT: Infisical /shared/controlplane-admin (prod); production control-plane boot hydration verifies canonical provenance, and operator clients receive it only for an authorized release
credentials.reader: molecule-controlplane AdminGateWithPromoteCapability verifies both exact routes; molecule-mcp-server promote_to_production and molecule-admin-cli Invoke-MolPromote currently call only POST /cp/admin/promote
credentials.notes: DISTINCT from CP_ADMIN_API_TOKEN: the control plane rejects an identical value at startup. The runtime-image route is further confined to the molecule-tenant template in production. Core/runtime must never source this capability into tenant boxes, and it must never be sent to staging.

credentials.grants: ONE fetch of the rendered /configs bundle at boot (GET /internal/workspaces/boot-config), then invalidated. No ongoing auth.
credentials.format: one-time single-use 256-bit hex; process-local in-memory store; TTL default 15m (MOLECULE_BOOT_CONFIG_TTL); superseded each reprovision

credentials.grants: pre-register boot-event phone-home (/cp/tenants/boot-event) and object-store platform-proxy restore-on-boot.
credentials.format: opaque stateless HMAC-SHA256 bearer, scoped + expiring — see contracts/boot-token

credentials.grants: metered LLM-proxy calls through the control plane.
credentials.format: opaque bearer (value currently sourced from the tenant admin_token)

routing.notes: aliases accepted by the mcp-server legacy surface only; management surface is strict MOLECULE_ORG_ID

the per-org tenant workspace-server host (<slug>.moleculesai.app), NOT the control plane. MOLECULE_PLATFORM_URL was an UNDECLARED name in real use — the SDK's own `python -m molecule_external_workspace connect` CLI reads it (__main__.py) and molecule-core's hermes-channel snippet exports it — found by building the core-side drift gate this contract has promised since sdk#78. Declared, not renamed: renaming it would break every operator who already exported it.
the control-plane host (api.moleculesai.app); distinct from tenant-url
falls back to org-slug in the mcp-server when unset

management_mcp_env._doc: Credential names understood by a privileged management MCP child (mcp_server_name=molecule-platform). The runtime forward-allowlist MUST carry management_mcp_env.required under exactly these canonical names (+ their aliases above). Optional names remain governed by each credential's holder/forbidden_on boundary: CP_PROMOTE_PROD_API_TOKEN is listed only for an operator-launched management MCP and core/runtime MUST NOT source or forward it into a tenant platform box or tenant concierge. This is the seam whose drift caused the concierge AUTH_ERROR.

_doc: Governs how a shown_once credential is rendered when its PLAINTEXT IS UNAVAILABLE — the re-show path.
A renderer (today: molecule-core's external-connect snippets) cannot just omit the credential site: an
empty string there yields material that LOOKS complete and 401s with no clue why. So it stamps
unavailable_marker instead, which makes the material visibly non-runnable. But 'visibly' only protects
the consumer that uses the credential INLINE — a bearer header 401s and nothing is lost.
For material that PERSISTS the credential — writes it to disk, registers it in a client's config store,
or restarts a live client onto it — the marker is just a string. The script runs happily and REPLACES
the holder's working credential with a dead one. Because the credential is shown_once, the real one is
then unrecoverable: recovery is a rotate. That is a destructive default, and it shipped (core#4315).
The rule lives HERE, in the credential SSOT, and not in the renderer: any future renderer of a
shown_once credential inherits it, and a consumer repo pins these three strings with a drift gate
instead of re-deriving them.
disclosure.guard_sentinel: molecule: this block has NO TOKEN

rules.applies_to: any renderer of a shown_once credential whose plaintext is unavailable
rules.rule: Stamp unavailable_marker at EVERY credential site. Never an empty string (looks complete, 401s silently), never a partial stamp (one site stamped, another hard-coded).

rules.applies_to: rendered material that PERSISTS the credential: writes it to a file, registers it in a client config store (`claude mcp add`, `openclaw mcp set`), or restarts a live client onto it
rules.rule: MUST fail closed on guard_needle: refuse to run, change NOTHING on disk, and emit guard_sentinel so the holder knows why. A guard MUST NOT terminate the surrounding session (the material is pasted into an interactive shell) — it skips rather than exits; a guard running inside a subprocess MAY exit non-zero.
rules.why: the credential is shown_once, so overwriting it with the marker destroys the only copy and forces a rotate

rules.rule: Assert the presence of guard_sentinel, NOT of guard_needle. unavailable_marker CONTAINS guard_needle, so a needle-presence assertion passes vacuously on completely unguarded material — a gate that cannot fail.
rules.why: core#4315 shipped because a gate asserted the marker was PRESENT rather than that anything ACTED on it

consumers.gate: TestSnippetCredentials_ConformToSDKCredentialsContract (vendored copy) + the re-vendor drift check in .gitea/workflows/e2e-external-connect-snippet.yml
